pr.title: Fix empty Zone / State / LGA dropdowns and unresponsive "I agree" checkbox
pr.body:
## Summary

Two bugs on `register_page.html` (and the related profile / login pages) fixed in this PR.

### 1. Empty Zone / State / LGA dropdowns (the originally reported bug)

PR #1 populated those selects dynamically from `ng_locations.js` after page load — when that script failed to load (e.g. opening the file directly via `file://`, strict cache/network policy, or any JS load error), the selects rendered with only the placeholder option.

Fix: follow the original `register_page.html` pattern (inline `<option>` tags) for all three selects.
- **Zone**: inline all 15 zones as `<option>` tags.
- **State**: inline all 37 states as `<option>` tags (restores the pre-PR-#1 pattern).
- **LGA**: inline all 774 LGAs grouped by state with `<optgroup label="State" data-state="State">`. A small `filterLgaByState(state)` helper hides non-matching `<optgroup>` elements when a state is selected.
- Removed the unused `<script src="ng_locations.js">` include and the `ng_locations.js` file itself.

Same change applied to `user_profile_page.html` Settings → Location.

### 2. "I agree" terms checkbox not clickable (follow-up bug)

When checking in the browser I found the terms checkbox didn't toggle. Root cause was a *pre-existing* bug in the original upload, surfaced once the form was actually exercised end-to-end:

```js
function tick(){
  const n=new Date();
  document.getElementById('clk').textContent = ...   // throws: #clk doesn't exist
}
tick(); setInterval(tick, 30000);
```

`#clk` is referenced but no `<… id="clk">` element exists on these pages. `tick()` therefore throws a `TypeError` at script init, halting the rest of the top-level script. The follow-on effect is that `let termsAccepted = false;` (declared further down) never executes, so when the user clicks the checkbox, `toggleTerms()` tries to read `termsAccepted` and hits the temporal dead zone — silently failing and never adding the `.checked` class.

Fix: make `tick()` a no-op when `#clk` isn't on the page. The rest of the top-level script now runs to completion, `termsAccepted` is initialized, and the checkbox toggles. Applied the same guard to `login_page.html` which had the identical pattern.

### Verified

- Loaded `register_page.html`, advanced to Step 2, opened the Zone / State / LGA dropdowns — all populated.
- Picked **Kano** as the state → LGA dropdown narrowed to Kano's 44 LGAs only.
- Clicked the "I agree" checkbox → orange checkmark appears, click again → unchecks.

![Step 2 with all dropdowns populated and the "I agree" checkbox checked](https://app.devin.ai/api/presigned_proxy?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..OAB4_XDS9rVRPmLTdsb-w2ObkMwGDuimNy1FftDHuoc)

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] Open `register_page.html`, fill Step 1 (name / phone / password), click **Continue to Location**, then confirm the Zone (15), State (37) and LGA (cascades by state) dropdowns are all populated.
- [ ] Click the "I agree to the Terms of Service…" checkbox — confirm the orange check appears. Click again — confirm it un-checks.
- [ ] Open `user_profile_page.html`, go to Settings → Location, confirm the same three dropdowns are populated and the State→LGA cascade works.

### Notes
- This is a follow-up on top of merged PR #1. Base is current `main`.
- The `#clk` element was never present in the original uploaded HTML — the `tick()` clock was unreachable from the start. This patch makes it gracefully no-op rather than poison the rest of the script. If a clock is desired later, add `<… id="clk">` somewhere on the page and `tick()` will populate it.
- `ng_locations.js` was removed since its data is now inline.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/d1f7dc0349894bc3a946e6e7402e2d49
Requested by: @manganadda-stalite
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
